steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content:
The passage explains that longitudinal studies like NEPS require extensive funding because they collect data “over timescales and at intervals” to track trends.

The “But” introduces a different study type—a transportation count seeking average daily usage—that is much less funding-dependent.

What makes this transportation study fundamentally different?

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A states that such studies “are not trying to identify trends over time.” A study measuring average daily usage avoids the repeated measurements across years that characterize longitudinal research.

The other choices discuss funding priority (B), participant recruitment (C), or scope expansion (D), none explaining the funding difference.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
